item.title: Fijian focused head of crucial Wales clash in Cardiff
item.description: With just two days remaining before Fiji takes on Wales in Cardiff in the Nations Championship, excitement is building as the Flying Fijians prepare for one of their biggest tests of the international season. The squad has been putting the finishing touches on its preparations, with coaches and players focused on delivering a disciplined and physical performance against a Welsh side eager to impre
item.content:encoded:
With just two days remaining before Fiji takes on Wales in Cardiff in the Nations Championship, excitement is building as the Flying Fijians prepare for one of their biggest tests of the international season.

The squad has been putting the finishing touches on its preparations, with coaches and players focused on delivering a disciplined and physical performance against a Welsh side eager to impress on home soil.

The build-up to the match has also highlighted the strong rugby ties between Fiji and the United Kingdom.

British Deputy High Commissioner to Fiji, Michael Moody, recently reaffirmed the UK's support for rugby development in Fiji through a donation to the Fiji Rugby Union today.

Moody says rugby has a unique ability to bring people together, creating lasting connections and strengthening communities across Fiji.

He says the contribution is intended to support Fiji Rugby's grassroots programmes, ensuring the sport remains accessible to young players where future talent is identified and developed.

He added that the United Kingdom is proud to support initiatives that encourage greater participation in rugby and inspire the next generation of Flying Fijians.

Moody says as Fiji prepares to run onto the field in Cardiff, the continued support for rugby development serves as a reminder that the country's success on the international stage is built on strong grassroots foundations,with hopes that the next generation will continue to carry the Flying Fijians to greater heights.

item.title: Love to start at flyhalf and Savea to captain All Blacks against France
item.description: 25-year-old Ruben Love has forced his way into the All Blacks starting XV when they host France in Christchurch this Saturday. Love, who was influential in the Hurricanes Super Rugby Pacific Grand Final win, will start at flyhalf, with Cam Roigard at halfback.
item.content:encoded:
25-year-old Ruben Love has forced his way into the All Blacks starting XV when they host France in Christchurch this Saturday.

Love, who was influential in the Hurricanes Super Rugby Pacific Grand Final win, will start at flyhalf, with Cam Roigard at halfback.

Head coach Dave Rennie has given the captaincy role to Ardie Savea, and has also named two potential Test debutants, Xavier Numia and Fehi Fineanganofo, who will be on the bench.

The starting XV features Ethan de Groot, Codie Taylor and Fletcher Newell in the front row, while Josh Lord and Sam Darry form the locking combination.

Peter Lakai and Luke Jacobson join captain Savea in the loose forwards.

Caleb Clarke and Will Jordan have been named on the wings, with Damian McKenzie selected at fullback.

Jordie Barrett and Quinn Tupaea will combine in the midfield.

Rennie's bench includes Asafo Aumua, Numia, Tyrel Lomax, Patrick Tuipulotu, Wallace Sititi, Cortez Ratima, Billy Proctor and Fineanganofo, with Numia and Fineanganofo both in line to make their Test debuts if called upon.

The All Blacks faces France this Saturday at 7.10pm in Christchurch and Australia takes on Ireland at 10.10pm, while South Africa will face England at 3.40am Sunday with Argentina playing Scotland at 7.10am.

The All Blacks will continue their Nations Championship campaign against Italy in Wellington on July 11th before hosting Ireland in Auckland on July 18th.

item.title: Knowledge fuels Flying Fijians' final preparations for Wales test
item.description: As the countdown begins with two more days till the FIJI Water Flying Fijians face Wales, the players are leaving no stone unturned in their preparations. Fiji Rugby Union says that beyond the physical demands of training, the squad has turned its focus to one of rugby's most important weapons—knowledge.
item.content:encoded:
As the countdown begins with two more days till the FIJI Water Flying Fijians face Wales, the players are leaving no stone unturned in their preparations.

Fiji Rugby Union says that beyond the physical demands of training, the squad has turned its focus to one of rugby's most important weapons—knowledge.

They say the Flying Fijians recently participated in a comprehensive Law Session led by former World Rugby 15s High Performance Match Officials Manager, Allain Rolland , giving players valuable insight into the modern interpretation of the game's laws.

FRU says as the international game continues to evolve, understanding how referees interpret breakdowns, tackle situations, offside lines and set-piece contests can often make the difference between winning and losing at the highest level.

They say the session provided players and coaches with a clearer understanding of current officiating trends, helping the team minimise costly penalties while maximising opportunities during crucial moments of the match.

Heading into the Wales clash, the Flying Fijians are combining their renowned physicality, flair and attacking instinct with a sharper understanding of the laws, a key ingredient in delivering an accurate and disciplined performance.

The Flying Fijians take on Wales at 1.10am Sunday.

They face England on July 12th at Hill Dickinson Stadium.

They will then wrap up their tour against Scotland on July 19th at Murrayfield Stadium.

In other matches this weekend, New Zealand will play France at 7.10pm Saturday in Christchurch and Australia takes on Ireland at 10.10pm, while South Africa will face England at 3.40am Sunday with Argentina playing Scotland at 7.10am.

item.title: USA book Round of 16 spot with 2-0 win over Bosnia and Herzegovina
item.description: The USA have booked their place in the Round of 16 of the FIFA World Cup after defeating Bosnia and Herzegovina 2-0 at the San Francisco Bay Area Stadium. Folarin Balogun opened the scoring for the USA in the 45th minute to give them a 1-0 lead at half-time.
item.content:encoded:
The USA have booked their place in the Round of 16 of the FIFA World Cup after defeating Bosnia and Herzegovina 2-0 at the San Francisco Bay Area Stadium.

Folarin Balogun opened the scoring for the USA in the 45th minute to give them a 1-0 lead at half-time.

The USA were reduced to 10 players in the second half after Balogun was shown a red card in the 64th minute.

Despite playing with a man down, they continued to push and sealed the win through Malik Tillman, who scored in the 82nd minute.

The win sees the USA advance to the knockout stage, where they will face Belgium in the Round of 16.

Meanwhile, Belgium defeated Senegal 3-2, while England booked their place in the next round with a 2-1 win over the Democratic Republic of the Congo and will face Mexico.

In tomorrow's Round of 32 matches, Austria will face Spain at 7am, Portugal will take on Croatia at 11am, while Switzerland will meet Algeria at 3pm.

item.title: Belgium edge Senegal 3-2 to reach Round of 16
item.description: Belgium staged a remarkable comeback from two goals down to beat Senegal 3-2 after extra time and book their place in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16. Senegal looked set to advance after goals from Habib Diarra and Ismaila Sarr gave them a deserved 2-0 lead with only minutes remaining in normal time.
item.content:encoded:
Belgium staged a remarkable comeback from two goals down to beat Senegal 3-2 after extra time and book their place in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16.

Senegal looked set to advance after goals from Habib Diarra and Ismaila Sarr gave them a deserved 2-0 lead with only minutes remaining in normal time.

However, substitute Romelu Lukaku pulled one back before Youri Tielemans headed in the equaliser in the 89th minute to make it 2-all and send the match into extra time.

Belgium completed the comeback in the 125th minute when Tielemans calmly converted a penalty after a VAR review ruled that Lamine Camara had fouled him inside the box.

Belgium will now face either the United States or Bosnia and Herzegovina in the Round of 16.

Meanwhile, England defeated the Democratic Republic of the Congo 2-1 to advance to the Round of 16, where they will face Mexico.

The United States will face Bosnia and Herzegovina at 12pm today .

item.description: Kylian Mbappe scored twice as France cruised to a 3-0 victory over Sweden this morning to book their place in the FIFA World Cup R
